</>
Internal Playbook · v1.0

AI-Assisted
Website Refresh.

YohDev's repeatable process for turning a sales handoff into a deployable client playbook — strategy, high-fidelity home page, and a block theme style guide — every time, in hours not weeks.

// Contents
  1. Process overview
  2. Sales → design handoff
  3. Discovery brief template
  4. The six-phase process
  5. Question bank
  6. Committing to a design system
  7. Build rules & anti-patterns
  8. The client playbook (deliverable)
  9. Quality rubric
  10. Deployment & handoff
// 00  overview

One brief in. One deployable playbook out.

The goal of this process is not to make a pretty mockup. It's to produce a self-contained, client-ready artifact — a Client Playbook — that captures strategy, shows the work, and hands off to the build team (or the client) with zero ambiguity. Everything we make is a static HTML site deployable to GitHub Pages.

INPUT

Sales Handoff

Discovery brief, brand assets, target audience, call notes, URL of current site.

from sales team
PROCESS

6-Phase Design

Intake → Research → System → Home Page → Style Guide → Package.

~4–8 focused hours
OUTPUT

Client Playbook

Zipped static site: strategy doc deep-linking to the home page mock and block theme style guide.

deploys to GH Pages
// 01  handoff

What sales hands to design.

Before a project enters the design queue, sales delivers a structured Sales Handoff package — eight Markdown files, zipped and attached to the HubSpot deal. If any are empty, the project is not ready. Send it back. Incomplete briefs are the single largest cause of bad design output.

01

Discovery Brief

Narrative summary of the discovery call: scope, timeline, success criteria, constraints, key quotes.

source · Otter + HubSpot
02

Brand Identity

Positioning, voice, personality axes, messaging pillars, words to use / avoid.

source · Otter + Phase 01 intake
03

Logo & Colors

Asset manifest with file paths. Logos (SVG preferred), palette, typography, brand guide link.

source · uploaded assets
04

Current Website

Live URL, platform, sitemap snapshot, pages in scope, tech / tracking inventory, known issues.

source · domain + /sitemap.xml
05

Target Audience

Primary + secondary audiences, anti-audience, how they decide, what makes them bounce.

source · Otter discovery
06

Personas

1–3 persona cards: role, jobs, obstacles, triggers, turn-offs, a direct quote.

source · Otter discovery
07

Strategy

The bet, the approach, guardrails, risks, what "done" looks like, open strategic questions.

source · Otter discovery
08

Client Profile

How to work with them. Communication style, decision-making, what they care about most, watch-outs.

source · Otter discovery
Rule
Don't design on guesses. The eight-file package is the contract between sales and design. Open Sales Handoff/ to see the boilerplate. Copy the folder per client, fill every {{placeholder}}, zip, attach to the deal.
// 02  intake

Turn the brief into design inputs.

Before the first pixel, convert the brief into a structured set of design decisions. This is the single most important phase — it's where the design gets its direction, before anyone is emotionally attached to a visual.

The 10-minute kickoff questionnaire

Run this with the client (or with yourself, if the brief is thorough) before you start. Every question should produce a concrete answer; "we'll see" is not an answer.

Direction

  • Overall vibe: evolution, modern refresh, or bold reimagining?
  • Color direction: keep existing, add a secondary, go lighter/darker?
  • Tone of voice: warm + human, technical + trustworthy, friendly-modern?
  • Photography: real assets, placeholders, or stock-style?
  • Any signature visual touches tied to the brand?

Scope

  • Which sections are must-have on the home page?
  • Interactivity: static mock, hover/tabs, or full prototype?
  • Variations: one polished direction, or 2–3 side-by-side?
  • What tweaks would be useful to toggle on the fly?
  • Anything to explicitly avoid?
Tip
Always ask at least 10 questions at intake. "Clean and simple polish" from the client is a directive — honor it literally. No filler sections, no padded copy, no invented data.
// 03  process

Six phases, always in order.

Every project walks this path. Skipping steps (especially the system commitment before building) is the fastest route to a mediocre mockup.

phase_01intake

Structured questions

Kickoff questionnaire. Lock direction, scope, tone, and variation count before researching. Produce a written summary of answers.

// produces
Intake summary · constraints list
phase_02research

Collect design context

Screenshot the current site. Pull brand assets into the project. List what's missing. If no design system exists, note that you'll derive one from visible cues (logo palette, voice, existing site vocabulary).

// produces
/assets folder · screenshot(s) · notes
phase_03system

Commit to a system out loud

Before building anything, write down the system: palette (with hex + role), type pairing (display + UI), spacing scale, signature motif. This is non-negotiable — it's what turns "pretty" into "coherent."

// produces
Design system decisions · inline in the HTML head
phase_04build

Home Page mock

One HTML file. Lean sections only — if the client said "simple," don't invent an FAQ. Signature motif threaded through 2–3 places. No filler content, ever.

// produces
Homepage.html + /assets
phase_05guide

Block Theme Style Guide

Translate the decisions from the mock into a handoff doc: color swatches with theme.json slugs, fluid type specimens, button variants, header + footer patterns, and a drop-in theme.json snippet.

// produces
Style Guide.html + theme.json
phase_06package

Client Playbook

A strategy overview page that deep-links to the home page mock and style guide. Zip the folder, deploy to GH Pages, hand off to the client.

// produces
Playbook zip · GH Pages URL
// 04  questions

The canonical ten.

Start every intake with these. Every one has produced a decision that changed the final design at least once. Add project-specific questions on top, never below.

  • 01 Overall vibe — evolution, modern refresh, or bold reimagining?
  • 02 Color direction — how do we treat the existing palette?
  • 03 Hero treatment — editorial split, full-bleed photo, centered, or CTA-first?
  • 04 Must-have sections — hero, services, case studies, team, contact?
  • 05 Tone of voice — warm + human, technical + trustworthy, friendly-modern?
  • 06 Interactivity — static, some hover/tabs, or fully prototyped?
  • 07 Variations — one polished direction, 2–3 side-by-side, or main + Tweaks?
  • 08 Signature visual touches — brand-rooted motifs, live widgets, illustration style?
  • 09 Photography — real team photos, placeholders, or stock-style?
  • 10 Must-avoids — anything the client explicitly rejects?
// 05  system

Write the system before you build.

Open the HTML file. In a comment at the top, declare the full system. If you can't, you're not ready to design. This single habit is the difference between coherent work and AI slop.

Palette

Pick a primary (brand-rooted, high-contrast on canvas), a soft secondary, a warm surface (canvas), an accent pulled from the logo, and neutrals. Never more than three brand colors. Define role + hex + a slug you'll use in theme.json.

Type pairing

Commit to one display/UI sans and one body sans. Use fluid clamp() sizes so the scale holds from 375px to 1920px without breakpoint juggling.

Signature motif

One visual element threaded through 2–3 places — a mark, a subtle texture, a type treatment. This is what makes the site feel designed, not assembled.

Anti-slop
No gradient backgrounds for their own sake. No emoji unless it's part of the brand. No left-border accent cards without purpose. No SVG illustration of imagery — use placeholders and request real assets. Less is more; one thousand no's for every yes.
// 06  build

Lean by default.

Every section must earn its place. If the brief says "clean and simple," the home page probably has five sections, not twelve. If a section feels empty, solve it with layout — never by inventing content.

✓

Match the vocabulary. Copy tone, color discipline, density, and shadow language stay consistent within a single file.

✕

No filler content. No dummy FAQs, no "Why Choose Us," no padded stat grids invented for visual balance.

✓

Real-feeling placeholders. Client names, case-study copy, and stats are plausible but explicitly noted as placeholders in the handoff.

✕

No data slop. Don't invent "98% satisfaction" or "500 projects shipped" unless the client gave you the number.

✓

Accessible by default. Body copy ≥ WCAG AA. Interactive elements have visible focus rings. Hit targets ≥ 44px on mobile.

✕

No SVG portraits pretending to be photos. Use stylized placeholders clearly, and flag real photography as a next step.

✓

Tweakable. Surface 3–5 toggles (density, accent color, hero variant) even if the client didn't ask — it opens conversations.

✕

No scope creep mid-build. Extra sections and pages happen in a v2 — ask the client first.

// 07  deliverables

The Client Playbook.

Every project ships as a single zipped static site. It's self-contained, deployable to GH Pages, and readable offline. Three files, one strategy narrative that links them together.

01 · NARRATIVE

Strategy Overview

One-pager that explains the approach, the audience insight, and the design decisions. Deep-links to the mock and style guide.

index.html
02 · VISION

Home Page Mock

High-fidelity, full-bleed, responsive. Contains Tweaks so the client can compare variants inline.

home/index.html
03 · HANDOFF

Block Theme Style Guide

Tokens, type specimens, buttons, header, footer, and a drop-in theme.json for a custom block theme.

style-guide/index.html

Folder structure

# {client-slug}-playbook/ ├── index.html # Strategy overview (entry point) ├── home/ │ ├── index.html # Home page mock │ └── assets/ # logo, images, fonts ├── style-guide/ │ ├── index.html # Block theme style guide │ └── theme.json # Drop-in for custom block theme ├── brief/ │ └── discovery.md # Sales handoff, archived ├── README.md # How to deploy to GH Pages └── .nojekyll # Prevents Jekyll on GH Pages

Strategy Overview — required sections

  • 01 Hello — a one-sentence framing of the project.
  • 02 What we heard — the client's priorities in their own words (from call notes).
  • 03 Strategy — our approach. Why this direction fits the audience.
  • 04 The Home Page — thumbnail + deep link. Three bullets on what's new.
  • 05 The Style Guide — thumbnail + deep link. What the dev team uses.
  • 06 Next steps — other pages, real photography, content migration.
// 08  quality

Ship checklist.

Run this before sending the client link. If any item fails, fix before delivery — not after.

  • system Palette, type, motif are declared at the top of each HTML file.
  • lean No filler sections, no invented stats, no dummy FAQs.
  • a11y Body copy passes WCAG AA. All interactive elements have focus rings.
  • tokens Style guide slugs match the theme.json palette exactly.
  • links Strategy Overview deep-links work locally and after GH Pages deploy.
  • assets Fonts load (local or Google CDN). Logo present. No broken images.
  • respond Looks correct at 1440, 1024, 768, 375 widths.
  • stubs Every placeholder (client name, stat, photo) is noted in the README.
  • readme Deploy instructions + next-step checklist are written.
  • archive The original Discovery Brief is in /brief/.
// 09  deploy

GH Pages in three commands.

Because everything is static HTML, the playbook deploys to GH Pages with zero build step. The .nojekyll file ensures folders starting with underscores still serve correctly.

# From inside {client-slug}-playbook/ git init git add . git commit -m "client playbook v1" git branch -M main git remote add origin git@github.com:yohdev/{client-slug}-playbook.git git push -u origin main # Then in the repo settings → Pages: # Source: Deploy from branch # Branch: main / (root) # Share with client: https://yohdev.github.io/{client-slug}-playbook/
Next
Once this playbook is stable, we'll layer a Claude Skill on top of it — a trigger prompt + instructions file that reproduces this exact process when invoked from the Discovery Brief. The playbook becomes the authoritative source the skill reads from.
YohDev · Internal Playbook · AI-Assisted Website Refresh v1.0 Maintained by the design team · yohdev.com
